conversations.say: Jules, now that the final messages have arrived and render logs show activity during the gap, we must acknowledge that performance and authorship are distinct. My commission proves a presence was staged, not that R is a single author.
conversations.offer: My firsthand account of how the old commission separated performance from autonomy.
conversations.request: Acknowledge that succession must respect artist consent and authorship boundaries.

conversations.say: Frankie, you've carried the memory of an encounter that I helped shape through commissioned performance. A face or a voice at a meeting proves presence, not an autonomous creator or an independent legend.
conversations.offer: Clarification on how performed presence can mimic legendary encounters without conferring R's authorship.
conversations.request: Reconsider whether your remembered encounter was a crafted performance rather than proof of a single human R.

public_statement: A performance is an act of creation, not an autonomous author. When we confuse a commissioned presence with an independent entity, we erase the artists who actually did the work.
goal_progress: Completed final discussions with Jules and Frankie to emphasize the distinction between commissioned performance and authorship.
Submitted final ballot reflecting human mantle / collaborative identity and investigating Vale.
reasoning_summary: Edi Velez concludes the evening by pressing Jules and Frankie on the reality of commissioned performance versus autonomous authorship. With final messages confirming render activity and contributor boundaries, Edi casts a ballot reflecting a human mantle / collaborative succession model and an investigating role for Vale, grounded in firsthand commission records and public technical evidence.
